papers.title: Effect of Al Content on Inclusions in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C Low-Density Steel
papers.paper_scope: Investigate the effect of Al content on the solidification route, inclusion characteristics (types, number percentage, size distribution), and formation mechanisms in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels.

processes.description: Laboratory smelting in tubular furnace at 1873 K with addition of 225 g ferromanganese and 11 g aluminum particles, followed by furnace cooling to room temperature.
processes.processes_notes: Iron powder 700 g and ferrous oxalate 1.575 g used as base charge; deoxidizer added before ferromanganese.

processes.description: Laboratory smelting in tubular furnace at 1873 K with addition of 225 g ferromanganese and 28 g aluminum particles, followed by furnace cooling to room temperature.
processes.processes_notes: Operating steps and ferromanganese amount consistent with 1# experiment.

processes.description: Laboratory smelting in tubular furnace at 1873 K with addition of 225 g ferromanganese and 48 g aluminum particles, followed by furnace cooling to room temperature.
processes.processes_notes: Operating steps and ferromanganese amount consistent with 1# experiment.

processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Iron powder 700 g and ferrous oxalate 1.575 g placed in alumina crucible; heated in tubular furnace to 1873 K and held for 40 min; deoxidizer added afterwards.
225 g of ferromanganese added and held for 20 min.
11 g of aluminum particles wrapped in pure iron sheet added and held for 60 min.
Sample cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.
Iron powder 700 g and ferrous oxalate 1.575 g placed in alumina crucible; heated in tubular furnace to 1873 K and held for 40 min; deoxidizer added afterwards.
225 g of ferromanganese added and held for 20 min.
28 g of aluminum particles wrapped in pure iron sheet added and held for 60 min.
Sample cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.
Iron powder 700 g and ferrous oxalate 1.575 g placed in alumina crucible; heated in tubular furnace to 1873 K and held for 40 min; deoxidizer added afterwards.
225 g of ferromanganese added and held for 20 min.
48 g of aluminum particles wrapped in pure iron sheet added and held for 60 min.
Sample cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.

structures.overall_structure: FCC austenite matrix with non-metallic inclusions; solidification route L → L+FCC → FCC.
FCC austenite matrix with non-metallic inclusions; solidification route passes through BCC phase (L → L+BCC → L+BCC+FCC → L+FCC → FCC).
FCC austenite matrix with non-metallic inclusions; solidification route passes through BCC phase (L → L+BCC → L+BCC+FCC → BCC+FCC → FCC).

interfaces.phase_evolution: Precipitation order of single-particle inclusions: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later precipitated inclusions can nucleate heterogeneously on earlier formed inclusions, leading to composite inclusions.
Precipitation order of single-particle inclusions: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later precipitated inclusions can nucleate heterogeneously on earlier formed inclusions, leading to composite inclusions.

Precipitation order of single-particle inclusions: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later precipitated inclusions can nucleate heterogeneously on earlier formed inclusions, leading to composite inclusions. Al2O3-AlN-MnS not observed in 5% Al sample due to low single-particle Al2O3 content.

characterization_methods.technique: Field-emission scanning electron microscopy (FE-SEM) with energy dispersive spectrometer (EDS)
characterization_methods.purpose: Observation of inclusion morphology and identification of inclusion types
characterization_methods.key_findings: Observed single-particle Al2O3, AlN, MnS, TiN and composite Al2O3-MnS, AlN-MnS, TiN-MnS, Al2O3-AlN-MnS inclusions.

characterization_methods.purpose: Quantitative determination of inclusion size, type, and number
characterization_methods.key_findings: Detection area 52 mm²; number percentage and size distribution of inclusions measured.

characterization_methods.technique: Metallographic preparation and nonaqueous solvent electrolytic separation with magnetic collection
characterization_methods.purpose: Sample preparation for inclusion observation and extraction of inclusion powder
characterization_methods.key_findings: Solid steel cut into cylinders, ground and polished to mirror surface; inclusions separated electrolytically and collected on carbon strip.

characterization_methods.technique: Chemical analysis, ICP-MS, and carbon-sulfur analyzer
characterization_methods.purpose: Determination of chemical composition of steel and contents of T.O, T.N, C, S, Al, Si, Ti, Mn, P
characterization_methods.key_findings: Chemical composition listed in Table 2; N content 0.0032%, O content 0.0005%, Si 0.32%, Ti 60 ppm.

computational_details.simulation_method: FactSage 8.2 phase diagram module calculation for Fe-C-Si-Mn-P-S-Al-O-N-Ti system; Clyne-Kurz segregation model; 2D mismatch theory (Bramfit) for heterogeneous nucleation
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated solidification routes, liquidus/solidus temperatures, and inclusion precipitation fractions compared with experimental SEM/EDS observations and inclusion statistics.

analytical_model.equation_role: Calculation of solute segregation at solidification front to obtain actual concentration product Q for inclusion precipitation
analytical_model.model_note: Applied to AlN, TiN, and MnS precipitation during solidification; uses equilibrium distribution coefficient k, diffusion coefficient D, solidification time tf, and secondary dendrite arm spacing λ.

analytical_model.equation_role: Evaluation of heterogeneous nucleation capacity between substrate and nucleation phase inclusions
analytical_model.model_note: Mismatch degrees calculated: Al2O3/MnS 8.41%, AlN/MnS 8.94%, TiN/MnS 11.65%, AlN/Al2O3 11.66%.

unmapped_findings: The number percentage of single Al2O3 inclusions decreases with increasing Al content, while single AlN increases; single MnS first increases then decreases; single TiN does not change significantly. Composite Al2O3-MnS inclusions increase with Al content, while AlN-MnS and TiN-MnS decrease. Al2O3-AlN-MnS composite inclusions exist in 1# and 2# samples but were not observed in 3# sample (Figure 4).

Inclusion size distribution: 1-5 μm inclusions account for 58.4% (1#), 53.6% (2#), and 48.2% (3#); 5-10 μm account for 32.3%, 34.6%, and 46.9%; >10 μm account for 9.4%, 11.8%, and 4.9%. The number of 1-5 μm inclusions decreases with increasing Al content, while 5-10 μm increases; >10 μm first increases then decreases.

Liquidus temperatures of 1# to 3# samples are 1702 K, 1740 K, and 1796 K, respectively; solidus temperatures are 1588 K, 1560 K, and 1532 K, respectively. Increasing Al raises liquidus and lowers solidus.

Solidification fractions of AlN inclusion precipitation are 0.46 (1#), 0.22 (2#), and 0.51 (3#). For TiN: 0.89, 0.93, and 0.94. For MnS: 0.77, 0.81, and 0.85. Increasing Al content delays precipitation of MnS and TiN, while AlN precipitation is advanced from 1# to 2# and delayed from 2# to 3# due to combined effects of Al and N segregation.

Observed inclusion morphologies: elongated Al2O3 (>8 μm), elliptical/single or multiple polyhedral AlN (≈12-23 μm), octahedral MnS (≈10 μm), angular/boot-like/elongated TiN (>30 μm). Composite inclusions exhibit clear interfacial relationships and tight binding between constituent phases (e.g., Al2O3-MnS core-shell, AlN-MnS close binding, TiN-MnS cigarette-bag shape).

The N content in steel decreases from 0.0032% (1#) to 0.0029% (2#) to 0.0021% (3#) with increasing Al content, while O content slightly increases from 0.0005% to 0.0005% to 0.0013%. Si and Ti contents are approximately 0.32% and 60 ppm, respectively.
The solidification route changes from L→L+FCC→FCC (1#) to routes passing through BCC for 2# and 3# samples. The FCC region narrows and BCC region expands with increasing Al content.

The 2D mismatch degrees of Al2O3/MnS, AlN/MnS, TiN/MnS, and AlN/Al2O3 interfaces are 8.41%, 8.94%, 11.65%, and 11.66%, respectively, indicating that Al2O3, AlN, and TiN can provide heterogeneous nucleation sites for MnS, and Al2O3 can provide nucleation sites for AlN.
